返回本批次

中国阻止 Meta 收购人工智能初创公司 Manus

China blocks Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus

www.cnbc.com 作者 yakkomajuri 分数 370 评论 268 2026/04/27 19:43
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 中国国家规划人员周一呼吁 Meta 终止对马努斯 (Manus) 的 20 亿美元收购。马努斯是一家具有中国血统的新加坡人工智能初创公司。国家发展和改革委员会在一份简短声明中表示，禁止外商投资马努斯的决定是根据法律法规作出的。该公司补充称，已要求相关各方撤回收购交易。Meta股价周一收高0.53%。该交易引起了中国和华盛顿的密切关注，因为美国立法者禁止美国投资者直接支持中国人工智能公司。与此同时，北京加大力度阻止中国人工智能创始人将业务转移到海外。现在观看中国政府对交易的干预引起了该国科技创始人和风险投资家的警惕，他们希望利用所谓的新加坡清洗模式，即公司从中国搬迁到城邦国家以避免北京和华盛顿的审查。马努斯在搬到新加坡之前在中国成立。该公司开发通用人工智能代理，并于去年 3 月推出了首款通用人工智能代理，可以执行市场研究、编码和数据分析等复杂任务。此次发布后，这家初创公司被誉为下一个 DeepSeek。Manus 表示，在推出产品 8 个月后，该公司的年度经常性收入 (ARR) 在 12 月就突破了 1 亿美元，并声称这使其成为当时世界上从 0 美元达到这一里程碑最快的初创公司。该公司去年 4 月在美国 VC Benchmark 领投的一轮融资中筹集了 7500 万美元。当 Meta 去年年底宣布这项交易时，这家科技巨头表示将寻求加速人工智能创新但今年 1 月，中国商务部表示将对此次收购是否符合出口管制、技术进出口和海外投资等方面的法律法规进行评估和调查。Meta 发言人告诉 CNBC，该交易“完全符合适用法律”，并预计“调查将得到适当解决”。 当被问及中国阻止 Meta 收购 Manus 的举动时，APEC 高官会议主席陈旭告诉记者，“重要的是各方本着互利的精神行事”。虽然陈旭表示他不了解问题的具体情况，但他表示，“如果这样的问题能够得到妥善处理，将有助于推动APEC进行更多实质性讨论。”这是根据官方英文翻译得出的。 — CNBC 的 Anniek Bao 和 Dylan Butts 对这个故事做出了贡献。

Original Summary

  1. China's state planner on Monday called for Meta to unwind its $2 billion acquisition of Manus, a Singaporean artificial intelligence startup with Chinese roots.The decision to prohibit foreign investment in Manus was made in accordance with laws and regulations, the National Development and Reform Commission said in a brief statement. It added that it has asked the parties involved to withdraw the acquisition transaction.Shares of Meta closed 0.53% higher on Monday.The deal had attracted scrutiny from both China and Washington, as lawmakers in the U.S. have prohibited American investors from backing Chinese AI companies directly. Meanwhile, Beijing has increased efforts to discourage Chinese AI founders from moving business offshore.watch nowThe Chinese government's intervention in the transaction drew alarm among tech founders and venture capitalists in the country who were hoping to take advantage of the so-called Singapore-washing model, where companies relocate from China to the city-state to avoid scrutiny from Beijing and Washington.Manus was founded in China before relocating to Singapore. The company develops general-purpose AI agents and launched its first general AI agent in March last year, which can execute complex tasks such as market research, coding and data analysis. The release saw the startup lauded as the next DeepSeek.Manus said it had passed $100 million in annual recurring revenue, or ARR, in December, eight months on from launching a product, which it claimed made it the fastest startup in the world at the time to hit the milestone from $0.The company raised $75 million in a round led by U.S. VC Benchmark in April last year.When Meta announced the deal late last year, the tech giant said it would look to accelerate artificial intelligence innovation for businesses and integrate advanced automation into its consumer and enterprise products, including its Meta AI assistant.But in January, China's Ministry of Commerce said it would conduct an assessment and investigation into how the acquisition complied with laws and regulations concerning export controls, technology import and export, and overseas investment.A Meta spokesperson told CNBC that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law," and that it anticipated "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."When asked about China's move to block Meta's acquisition of Manus, APEC Senior Officials Meeting Chairman Chen Xu told reporters that it is "important that all parties act in a spirit of mutual benefit."While Chen said he did not know the specifics of the issue, he said that "if such an issue can be handled properly, it can help facilitate more substantive discussions in APEC." That's according to an official English translation.— CNBC's Anniek Bao and Dylan Butts contributed to this story.

评论（默认中文）

maxglute 2026/04/28 04:18
这只是中华人民共和国最终应用了他们版本的美国出口管制，即中华人民共和国开始控制源自中华人民共和国的算法，这与 TikTok 的论点相同。创始人没有被扣为“人质”，他们因违反出口管制和国家安全法而受到调查。 PRC 暗示，在 Manus 交易完成之前，他们将使用出口管制法第 12 条（一揽子条款）和离岸附属公司规则（以解决新加坡漏洞），但 Manus 忽略了响亮的暗示。不同之处在于，中国在执行与人工智能相关的出口管制方面并不是非常明智（特别是因为代理开发是新的，因此第 12 条），美国将确保该控制清单技术不会通过外国产品规则/CFIUS/BIS 离开美国领土。 PRC向马努斯发出了非常明确的信号，告诉马努斯将会发生什么，希望他们能够自行放松，但他们没有，所以现在他们要吃屎了。

中国仍未像美国禁止中国半导体那样禁止中国战略人才在美国工作。不要感到惊讶，在 5-10 年内，美国必须雇用身份模糊的中国工人，就像中国在中国 EUV 中处理美国/台湾人才一样。这些事情的升级还有很大的空间，具体取决于中华人民共和国开始对待双重用途人工智能的认真程度。

This just PRC finally applying their version of US export controls, i.e. PRC gets to control PRC originated algos, same argument as TikTok. The founders aren't held "hostage", they're under investigation for violating export control and national security laws. PRC hinted signalled pretty clearly they would use art12 (catch all clause) of export control laws and offshore affiliate rules (to address Singapore loophole) before Manus deal closed - Manus ignored loud hints. The difference is PRC wasn't super judicious in enforcing AI related export controls (especially since agent development new hence art12), US would have ensured this control list tech wouldn't leave US territory via foreign product rule / CFIUS / BIS. PRC gave pretty clear signals to Manus what was going to happen hoping they'd unwind on their own, but they didn't so now they're going to eat shit.

PRC still haven't gone the step up to ban PRC strategic talent from working in US like US has for PRC semi. Don't be surprised in 5-10 years US has to hire PRC workers with obfuscated identities like PRC dealing with US/TW talent in PRC EUV. Plenty more room how these things can escalate depending on how serious PRC starts to treat dual use AI.

maxglute 2026/04/28 12:28
哦，在调查基本上是叛国罪的过程中，不要拿护照作为飞行风险……打倒……世界上每个政府。

无论如何，对于中国的出口管制，“堵人”也不是错误的结论。对于那些想知道中华人民共和国可以采取什么措施的人来说。中国没有美国 80 多年的域外工具，即银行业。但中国确实拥有大量人才，即全球人工智能的 50 多名，很快将占全球半导体的 50% 以上。

根据几周前国务院颁布的关于打击外国非法外星管辖措施的新规定，人才似乎将受到控制。 IIRC 第 834/5 号法令。在 Manus 宣布之前它被提前加载是​​有原因的。 TLDR 规定，中国公民在中华人民共和国列入恶意实体名单的任何美国/外国公司工作均属非法。

从理论上讲，这会切断愿意为目标公司工作的大量中国人才，除非公司花费美元来保证绿卡，因为在这些公司工作或与个人（包括 Manus 创始人，如果他们的计划成功）（即 Facebook）有联系的中国公民基本上会犯下叛国罪。一大堆绿卡，因为需要买断所有利益相关者，也就是说，它会杀死整个中国团队/公司的挖角，任何受影响公司的中国人才渠道都会消失。别介意胁迫家庭，许多有能力的中国人才拥有数百万的房地产/资产，如果不是个人的话，那么通过继承，一切都消失了。问问如果没有大量中国一线人才，美国的人工智能或科技会在哪里。当然，有些人会认为这是一件好事（消除风险等）。

Oh no taking passport for flight risks during investigation for basically treason... down with... every government in the world.

Anyway that said, with respect to PRC export controls "barring people" is also not wrong conclusion persay. For those down in the thread wondering what PRC can do about it. PRC doesn't have US 80+ years tier of extraterritorial instrument, i.e. banking. But PRC does have fuckload of talent, i.e. 50+ of global AI, soon 50%+ of global semi.

Seems like talent is going to get controlled according to new rules on countering foreign states unlawful extraterrestrial jurisdiction measures from state council a couple weeks ago. IIRC decree 834/5. There's a reason it was front loaded before Manus announcement. TLDR make it illegal for Chinese nationals to work in any US/foreign company that PRC puts on malicious entity list.

In theory, this cuts off huge % of PRC talent willing to work for targeted companies, unless companies spend $$$ guaranteeing green cards, because PRC nationals working in these companies or affiliating with individuals (including Manus founders if their scheme worked out), i.e. Facebook, would basically be committing treason. A fuckload of greencards because need to buy out all stakeholders, i.e. it kills poaching entire PRC teams / companies, any PRC talent funnel dead for affected companies. Nevermind coercing families, many competent PRC talent has millions in RE/assets, if not personally then via inheritance, all gone. Ask where US AI or tech would be without tons of tier1 PRC talent. Of course some will argue this good thing (derisking etc).

在 HN 中查看
nsoonhui 2026/04/28 10:34
我认为这与出口管制没有太大关系——请注意，马努斯尽管令人印象深刻，但仍然是基本西方模式的包装——相反，它与资本管制有更多关系。

出于当地政治方面的考虑，中国长期以来一直试图阻止企业和个人大规模外流。马努斯所做的事情，首先在中国取得了成功，然后在新加坡设立了一家名义上的空壳公司，这似乎是一个教科书般的逃亡案例，而中国正试图阻止这种情况发生。

I don't think this has much to do with export control-- note that Manus, as impressive as it is, is still a wrapper around fundamental western models--, rather it has more to do with capital controls.

China has been trying to stop large scale outflow of businesses and individuals for quite some time, due to local politics concern. What Manus was doing, achieving successes first in China then setup a nominal shell company in Singapore, seems like a textbook case of flight (润）, which China is trying to prevent.

在 HN 中查看
bluegatty 2026/04/28 10:59
这是一个很好的答案。出口管制有一个战略目的——马努斯完全符合管制的精神，但也许不符合规则的技术性。

考虑一下，如果这是一个小得多的项目，他们会遇到相同的技术细节，但他们会受到制裁吗？可能不会。

对这些规则在不同政权中的任意应用进行比较是非常公平的，天知道“TikTok”被当作皮纳塔对待，但仍然认为这是关于“某些规则”的天真。这是关于“盛大游戏”的。

应该注意的是：我认为“名义外壳”的东西对于所有国家来说都是值得仔细审查的。我认为所有这些“加勒比岛公司”无论如何都违反了商业法和惯例的“精神”。如果马努斯“确实”是一家新加坡公司，那是一回事，但它实际上只是“一些文书工作”，这为所应用的“境内规则”提供了合法性。

This is a good answer. The export controls have a strategic purpose - and Manus fits squarely within the spirit of the controls and maybe not the technicality of the rules.

Consider that if this were a much smaller project, they'd run afoul of the same technicalities but would they be sanctioned? Probably not.

It's very fair to make comparisons as to the arbitrary application of these rules in various regimes, lord knows 'TikTok' has been treated like a Pinata, but still, it'd be naive to think that this is about 'some rule'. It's about the 'Grand Game'.

Should note: the 'nominal shell' stuff I think is fair game for all nations to be scrutinizing. All of this 'Caribbean Island Incorporation' I think violates 'the spirit' of commercial laws and practices anyhow. It'd be one thing if Manus was 'really' a Singapore company but that it's truly just 'some paperwork' gives legitimacy to the 'onshore rules' being applied.

wxw 2026/04/28 03:21
> 2025 年 5 月，在美国风投公司 Benchmark 领投的一轮 7500 万美元融资后，Manus 于 7 月关闭了中国办事处，并解雇了数十名员工。随后，该公司将业务转移至新加坡。

> 目前尚不清楚中国以何种理由寻求撤销涉及一家新加坡公司的交易，以及如何撤销已完成的收购交易（如果有的话）。

> 五位知情人士称，Manus 的两位联合创始人、首席执行官肖红和首席科学家季一超于 3 月份被监管机构传唤到北京进行会谈，随后被禁止出境。

看看这会如何发展将会很有趣。

> After a $75 million fundraising round led by U.S. venture firm Benchmark in May 2025, Manus shut its China offices in July, laying off dozens of employees. It then moved its operations to Singapore.

> It was not immediately clear on what grounds China was seeking the annulment of a deal involving a Singapore-based company and how, if at all, a completed acquisition transaction would be unwound.

> Manus' two co-founders, CEO Xiao Hong and chief scientist Ji Yichao, were summoned to Beijing for talks with regulators in March and later barred from leaving the country, five sources familiar with the matter said.

Will be interesting to see how this plays out.

kelnos 2026/04/28 06:25
第三个引用似乎使第二个引用无效，不是吗？ 根据关键人物目前身在中国的“理由”，中国政府可以强迫他们为所欲为。

尽管我认为如果这两个人没有公司的多数股权，那么实际的（前）多数股东可以拒绝解除出售，无论他们的意愿如何。 不过，如果没有这些关键人物，公司对 Meta 的价值可能会大大降低。 不管怎样，我认为滞留在中国的两个人不会看到售价的一毛钱，这并不酷。

（这与我对 Meta 拥有更多 AI 能力的感受无关……）

The third quote seems to invalidate the second, no? Under the "grounds" that key people are currently physically in China, and as such, the Chinese government can coerce them to do whatever it wants.

Though I suppose if those two did not have majority ownership of the company, the actual (former) majority owners can refuse to unwind the sale regardless of their wishes. Company might be worth quite a bit less to Meta without those key people, though. Either way, I assume the two people stuck in China won't be seeing a dime of that sale price, which is not cool.

(This is regardless of my feelings about Meta owning more AI capability...)

两位联合创始人将无法为 Meta 工作。在香港发行《Manus》可能会很复杂，新加坡也可能如此。

The two cofounders will not be able to work for Meta. Probably it will be complicated to distribute Manus in Hong Kong, possibly Singapore too.

But Manus's IP was already transferred and in any case Meta is not legally doing business in China, so Manus will still live on, possibly get rebranded.

giwook 2026/04/28 04:26
不知何故，我认为确实有可能发生更多事情。

对于那些没有被指控犯有任何罪行的人来说，禁止他们离开这个国家感觉有点险恶。

我并不声称知道所报道的内容之外发生了什么，但我想起了其他一些“越界”（由北京确定）的人，他们要么被禁止进入该国，要么一次神秘失踪数周或数月，只是在某个时候随机出现，唱着不同的曲调。

Somehow I think there is a real possibility more will happen.

Barring them from leaving the country feels a bit sinister for people who haven't been accused of committing any crimes.

I don't claim to know what's going on outside of what's being reported, but I'm reminded of other individuals who have "stepped out of line" (as determined by Beijing) and were also either barred from the country or mysteriously disappeared for weeks or months at a time only to randomly reappear at some point singing a different tune.

culi 2026/04/28 13:33
违反出口规则。技术工作者应该习惯“发明转让协议”的想法。

Manus 是在中国建造的，它的所有开发都发生在中国。为了避开中国对该交易的审查，他们试图关闭那里的商店并搬到新加坡。

我不认为中国是无理取闹的。我相信，如果一家美国科技公司从中国筹集资金，然后试图在美国倒闭，并将其所有知识产权和技术转移到另一个国家，以便阿里巴巴或字节跳动无需美国批准就可以收购它，美国也会采取完全相同的做法

Breaking the export rules. Tech workers should be used to the idea of a "Invention Assignment Agreement".

Manus was built in China and all of its development happened there. In order to skirt Chinese review of the deal they tried to close down shop there and move to Singapore.

I don't think China is being unreasonable. I'm sure the US would act exactly the same way if an American tech company raised money from China and then tried to close down in the US and move all of its IP and technology to a different country so that it can be bought out by Alibaba or Bytedance without having to deal with US approval

除了移民问题之外，只有当您因犯罪被捕并被起诉时，作为保释的一部分，您才会被要求交出护照。该权力只能由法官授予。

nsagent 2026/04/28 08:56
第一个案例是有道理的：前中央情报局官员明确揭露中央情报局官员。当然，政府会介入，与限制随机公民相比，这是一种错误的等同。

至于你的第二个案例，美国学校教授麦卡锡主义的危险。您忽略了最高法院随后于 1958 年做出的裁决，该裁决推翻了因言论受保护而没收护照的决定。请注意那是多久以前的事了，以及它是如何被当作美国历史上的一个黑色污点来教导的。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kent_v._Dulles

The first case makes sense: ex-CIA officer explicitly outing CIA officers. Naturally, the government is going to step in and it's a false equivalence to compare to restricting random citizens.

As for your second case, US schools teach about the perils of McCarthyism. You neglected to link to the subsequent Supreme Court ruling in 1958 overturning the confiscation of the passport over protected speech. Note how long ago that was and how it's taught as a black stain on US history.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kent_v._Dulles

在 HN 中查看
smcin 2026/04/28 11:11
在美国，自 1998 年起实施护照拒绝计划（其他发达国家也颁布了类似立法），遵循 1992 年《联合国儿童权利公约》（UNCRC）[2]：

> 子女抚养费强制执行护照拒绝计划是作为 1996 年《个人责任与工作机会协调法案》的一部分而制定的。该计划于 1996 年获得授权，并于 1998 年 6 月由美国国务院和联邦儿童抚养费强制执行办公室联合实施。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Child_support#Enforcement

[1]：“[美国]子女抚养费强制执行护照拒绝计划” https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/IN12660

[2]： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._ratification_of_the_Conve...

In the US, the Passport Denial Program, since 1998 (other developed countries enacted similar legislation), following the 1992 UN Convention on the Rights of the Child (UNCRC) [2]:

> The Child Support Enforcement Passport Denial Program was enacted as part of the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996. While authorized in 1996, the program was jointly implemented by the U.S. Department of State and the Federal Office of Child Support Enforcement in June 1998.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Child_support#Enforcement

[1]: "The [US] Child Support Enforcement Passport Denial Program" https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/IN12660

[2]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._ratification_of_the_Conve...

在 HN 中查看
andersonpico 2026/04/28 07:29
> 中国在没有任何此类事情或任何正当程序的情况下任意将人们困在中国。

是什么让您认为没有法律程序来阻止国民离开中国？这是一种非常常见的工具，在许多国家，这是一种行政措施，其严格程度甚至比司法授权还要少。您认为法国或英国是没有法治或正当程序的国家吗？

但在美国，例如，政府可以签发逮捕令让你作为重要证人，或者标记你的护照，然后你就不能离开；这些几乎不是正当程序，而更像是做同样事情的法律变通办法；然而，美国以比这更险恶的方式让很多人消失了，所以我同意这里没有对等物：美国更糟糕。

> China arbitrarily traps people in China without any such thing or any due process whatsoever.

What makes you think there's no legal process for blocking nationals from leaving China?It's a very common instrument and in a bunch of countries it's an administrative measure with even less scrutinity than a judicial mandate. Do you consider France or the UK to be a countries without rule of law or due process?

But to the point in the US, for example, the government can just issue a warrant for you as a material witness or flag your passport and then you can't leave; these are hardly due processes and more like legal workarounds to do exactly the same thing; the US has disappeared plenty of people in much more sinister ways than that, however, so I agree that there's no equivalence here: the US is worse.

guywithahat 2026/04/28 05:08
> 对于那些没有被指控犯有任何罪行的人来说，禁止他们离开这个国家感觉有点险恶

我认为这在中国实际上并不少见，尤其是对于高调的人来说。值得中国赞扬的是，如果人们被指控犯罪但没有被定罪，我们经常禁止他们出境。虽然这确实令人恐惧和专制，但我认为这在中国是正常的。大多数公司都有一定程度的中共代表，无论是在董事会还是在一定级别的管理层。

> Barring them from leaving the country feels a bit sinister for people who haven't been accused of committing any crimes

I don't think it's actually that uncommon in China, especially with high profile people. To China's credit, we often bar people from leaving the country if they're charged with a crime but not convicted of anything. While it's certainly scary and authoritarian, I think it's par for the course in China. Most companies have some amount of CCP representation in them, either on the board or some level of management.

skippyboxedhero 2026/04/28 06:00

每个人都有民族主义的唯我论，这使他们无法理解发生在自己国家之外的事件。中国和美国是这种情况最严重的两个国家，这些国家以外的人似乎认为他们对那里发生的事件有着深刻的、天生的理解，使他们能够提供完整的意见（而实际上，这种意见几乎总是完全自我指涉的，这个帖子上20%的评论似乎都在谈论美国）。

Yes, everyone country does this. You can be barred from travel in a wide range of other circumstances in many other countries.

Every person has a nationalistic solipsism that renders them incapable of understanding events that occur outside of their own country. China and the US are two countries where this tends to be most severe, people outside these countries seem to believe they possess a profound and innate understanding of events there that renders them capable of offering a complete opinion (and, in reality, that opinion will almost always be entirely self-referential, 20% of the comments on this thread seem to be talking about the US).

At a high-level, the characterization of China as a lawless dictatorship is undermined somewhat by the higher levels of crime in almost every other country. You will see this interpretation of China from people in the US who live in places where there are constant, visible signs of crime.

你又回到了“什么主义”。这是无关紧要的。如果我们在中世纪进行类似的辩论，你可能会说这样的话：

> 每座教堂都将女巫和异端分子烧死在火刑柱上。这样做是教会的核心职能。

中共绑架了这些人，并阻止他们出境。这不行。你不能通过说“是的，但他们是政府，所以他们有权绑架任何他们想要的人”来证明这一点。政府只是一个比其他人拥有更多权力的公司，而不是高于我们的神圣实体。

A government is just a corporation with a bit more power than the others, not some sacred entity that sits above us.

Fricken 2026/04/28 08:06
>政府只是一个比其他人拥有更多权力的公司，而不是高于我们的神圣实体。

是的，政府并不需要神圣才能高于你，它只需要拥有更多的权力。它的合法性取决于维持对暴力的垄断。

>A government is just a corporation with a bit more power than the others, not some sacred entity that sits above us.

mothballed 2026/04/28 08:23
幸运的是，中国周围有许多第三世界国家的陆地边界，边界漏洞百出，其中很多国家都没有人对一些贫穷的中国村民过境太在意。 另一方面，美国人有加拿大，就近地轨道而言，基本上是美国的延伸，而墨西哥，由于毒品贸易和其他独特因素，意味着任何越过边境被抓到的人都可能欠卡特尔一大笔钱或一些极其不受欢迎的好处。

我绝对宁愿做一个被困试图逃跑的中国人，也不愿做一个被困的美国人。

Luckily china has a litany of 3rd world countries land borders surrounding it with porous borders, and in a great deal of them no one who gives too many shits about some poor chinese villager crossing. Americans on the other hand have Canada which for LEO purposes is basically an extension of the US, and Mexico which due to the drug trade and other unique factors mean anyone getting caught jumping the border in either direction is likely to owe the cartel a massive amount of money or some extremely undesirable favors.

I would definitely rather be a trapped Chinese trying to escape than a trapped American.

komali2 2026/04/28 13:29

在 HN 中查看
glerk 2026/04/28 08:17
如果我们要陷入迂腐，我的陈述是规范性的，而不是描述性的，就像“我同意这是政府所做的，我不同意这是政府应该做的”。

“在我之下”是我对这个政府及其附属机构的价值判断，就像“它已经被权衡过，被发现不值得”。这个政府比我拥有更多的权力，但这并不意味着它在道德上绝对高于我，也没有神奇地赋予它合法性。

If we’re going to descend into pedantry, my statement was normative, not descriptive, as in “I agree this is what a government does, I disagree this is what it _should_ do”.

“Beneath me” is _my_ value judgement that I pass on this government and its appendages as in “it has been weighed in the balance and has been found unworthy”. That this government has more power than me doesn’t make it sit above me as a moral absolute, and it doesn’t magically give it legitimacy.

在 HN 中查看
Fricken 2026/04/28 08:21
政府位于你之上，因为它让你在暴力威胁下做事。为什么停在停车标志处？因为如果您不这样做，政府保留伤害您的权利。

政府的合法性来自于它的棍子比你的大。它不是神圣的，也不是魔法的。这是一根更大的棍子。如果你的棍子更大，你的价值判断就会有分量。拿着更大棍子的人决定你（或马云）值得什么。

The government sits above you because it makes you do things under the threat of violence. Why do you stop at the stop sign? because the government reserves the right to hurt you if you don't.

The government's legitimacy comes from it's stick being bigger than yours. It's not sacred, it's not magic. It's a bigger stick. Your value judgement would have weight if your stick was bigger. The guy with the bigger stick decides what you (or Jack Ma) is worthy of.

在 HN 中查看
glerk 2026/04/28 09:38
> 政府的合法性来自于它的棍子比你的大

按照同样的论点，索马里军阀和墨西哥贩毒集团在他们控制的领土上也合法吗？我不认为“合法”是你正在寻找的描述纯粹权力动态的词，因为“合法”充满了道德判断（查找是与应该等）。但是，是的，在实践中，如果有人用枪指着我的头，我可能会被迫做一些违背我判断的事情（在限度内！）。

> The government's legitimacy comes from it's stick being bigger than yours

By the same argument, are Somalian warlords and Mexican drug cartel also legitimate in the territories they control? I don't think "legitimate" is the word you are looking for to describe pure power dynamics, since "legitimate" is imbued with a moralistic judgement (look up is vs ought etc.). But yes, in practice, if I have a gun pointed at my head, I could be forced to do things that go against my judgement (within limits!).

mothballed 2026/04/28 05:35
即使没有犯罪，这种情况也很常见。 例如，美国会阻止或取消子女抚养债务极少的人（我认为 1000 美元，这对于中产阶级来说是单次拖欠付款）和税收债务中等的人（我认为大约 25,000 美元）获得护照。

ericmay 2026/04/28 05:22

但值得一提的是，这不是美国的运作方式，也不是我们的法治方式。

They do have export control laws and such, but based on current and past behavior China’s Communist Party doesn’t need those laws to disappear people or create crimes and then make people guilty of said crimes.

Worth mentioning though that this is not how America functions, nor our rule of law.

rvnx 2026/04/28 06:49

在 HN 中查看
cataphract 2026/04/28 07:24
美国是一个民主国家，人们被赋予了很多程序性和实质性的权利，即使是关塔那摩的被拘留者（我们可以说布迈丁是否有任何实际效果，但我们不会在中国看到同样的权利）。

但美国人的印象是，世界所看到的正是他们最常看到的——国内方面。在某种程度上，它们确实得益于其文化影响力。然而，这种民主/法治在境外的表现却完全不存在，现在每个人都比以往任何时候都更加清楚，美国是世界上最大的不稳定根源。比俄罗斯还多。肯定比中国多。

The US is a democracy, and people are given many procedural and substantive rights, even Guantanamo detainees (we can argue if Boumediene had any practical effect, but we wouldn't have seen the same from China).

But Americans are under the impression that what the world sees is what they mostly see -- the domestic side. And to a certain extent, they do thanks to its cultural influence. This democracy/rule of law, however, is completely absent in way it behaves outside its borders and it's now clearer than ever to everyone that the US is the biggest source of instability in the world. More than Russia. Certainly more than China.

rvnx 2026/04/28 12:17
也许是爱泼斯坦？

“美国目前没有计划对爱泼斯坦案件进行新的审判，因为没有可靠的证据”

法律的适用实际上取决于您有多少钱。

我们在公司中也看到了这种情况，比如如果你是 OpenAI 或 Nvidia，复制盗版作品突然变得可以了。

有钱人赔钱，穷人进监狱。

庭外和解是这种情况的主要定义。

从技术上讲，是的，法律得到遵守。

您可以正式向德克萨斯州的法官赠送礼物也是如此。

在这个星球上的任何地方，无论纸上写了什么，有关系的人都可以影响结果，美国也不例外。

Epstein maybe ?

“No new US trials are currently planned for the Epstein cases because there has not been credible evidence“

Application of the laws genuinely depends of how much money you have.

We also see it with companies, like if you are OpenAI or Nvidia it suddenly becomes ok to copy pirated works.

Rich people pay damages, poor people go to prison.

Out-of-court settlements are prime definition of such.

Technically, yes the law is followed.

The same with gifts you can officially make to judges in Texas.

Anywhere on this planet where people who have connections can influence the outcome, no matter what is written on a paper, and the US is not exempt.

在 HN 中查看
stego-tech 2026/04/28 03:33
我怀疑这更多的是对其他试图采取同样策略的人的警告（我听人们称之为“新加坡清洗”）：国家正在注视着，不断变化的地缘政治意味着在国内保留成功的人才和实体而不是让反对派拥有它们，这符合他们的利益。

如果有的话，我真的很惊讶他们花了这么长时间。 美国几十年来一直这样做，但没有受到太多反击，因此中国必须对自己使用这种策略的立场非常有信心。

I suspect this is more of a warning shot to others attempting the same playbook ("Singapore-washing", as I've heard folks call it): the state is watching, and shifting geopolitics means it's in their interest to retain successful talent and entities at home rather than let opposition have them.

If anything, I'm genuinely surprised it took them this long. America's been doing this for decades without much in the way of pushback, so China must feel very confident in its position to use such tactics.

aesch 2026/04/28 03:38
我不知道美国有没有做过类似的事情。我正在寻找的例子是，一家公司在美国成立，但离开并在美国境外注册，然后美国试图阻止外国公司收购。此外，执行机制虽然模糊，但似乎不美国。美国可能会在公司退出时对其征税，但不会将创始人扣押在美国。如果你有例子我会很好奇。

The example I'm looking for is where a company starts in the US but leaves and incorporates outside the US and then the US attempts to block acquisition by a foreign company. Also, the enforcement mechanism while vague seems un-American. America might tax the company upon exit but it wouldn't hold the founders hostage in America. If you have examples I'd be curious.

DontBreakAlex 2026/04/28 05:29
您无需在美国注册公司即可发生这种情况。你应该看看马克·拉苏斯（Marc Lasus）创立 Gemplus 后发生了什么（剧透，他靠社会保障生活，而中央情报局从他那里偷走的公司却有 30 亿美元的收入），或者弗雷德里克·皮耶鲁奇 (Frédéric Pierucci) 如何被扣为人质，迫使法国将核反应堆出售给通用电气。我认为美国也会对所有其他国家这样做。
You don't need to incorporate in the US for this to happen to you. You should look up what happened to Marc Lasus after he founded Gemplus (spoiler, he's on social security while the company the CIA stole from him has $3b revenue) or how Frédéric Pierucci was taken hostage to force the sale of France's nuclear reactors to General Electric. I assume the US does this to all the other countries too.

capitalhilbilly 2026/04/28 05:36
这么晚被拦住和美国AFAIK有点不同， 但肯定有可能被停止工作并且（取决于你如何反应）被阻止离开美国进行纯粹的经济发明：

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Invention_Secrecy_Act

我发现值得注意的是，美国对政府的实际检查不利于将保密法进一步扩大到对受惠行业的经济保护主义等。

Being stopped that late is a bit different than the US AFAIK, but there is certainly the possibility of being stopped from work and (depending how you react) prevented from leaving the US for purely economic inventions:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Invention_Secrecy_Act

I find it notable that the US' actual checks on government have worked against expanding the secrecy act further into economic protectionism for favored industries, etc.

vkou 2026/04/28 05:00
美国不需要做“这样的事情”，如果他们不喜欢你，他们可以禁止你进入全球金融体系。 [1]

或者干脆命令另一个国家来抓你。

> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meng_Wanzhou

她被捕，并被从加拿大引渡到美国……因为她的中国公司正在与伊朗做生意。

> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viktor_Bout

这个家伙在泰国被捕，被引渡，并在美国监狱关了十年，因为他胆敢从俄罗斯向哥伦比亚出售武器。我不确定美国法律与此类交易到底有何关系......

---

[1] 或者，自 2025 年起，只需向你的船发射一枚导弹，如果有幸存者，可以选择进行后续齐射。奇怪的是，迄今为止，所有在最初的攻击和双击中幸存下来的人都已被遣返回原籍国，美国没有提出任何指控。

The US doesn't need to do 'something like this', they can just bar you from the global financial system if they don't like you. [1]

Or just order another country to snatch you up.

> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meng_Wanzhou

She was arrested, and was being extradited from Canada into the United States... Because her Chinese company was doing business with Iran.

> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viktor_Bout

This chap was arrested in Thailand, extradited, and did a decade in a US prison because he had the audacity of selling weapons from Russia to Colombia. I'm not sure how exactly US law is of any relevance to such transactions...

---

[1] Or, since 2025, just shoot a missile at your boat, with an option for a follow-up salvo if there any survivors. Strangely enough, everyone who has managed to survive both the initial attack, and the double-tap has so far been repatriated to their countries of origin, with no charges filed by the US.

在 HN 中查看
cermicelli 2026/04/28 05:27
美国阻止公司合并，特别是与中国和其他非西方公司的合并。包括日本、印度等。

例如，日本新日铁公司收购美国钢铁公司就是这样的例子。 https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2vz83pg9eo

更多例子，

蚂蚁集团（中文）试图收购速汇金（2018年被阻止） https://www.reuters.com/article/business/us-blocks-moneygram...

厦门三安光电试图收购Lumileds，再次遭到美国阻挠。还有中国的ofc。

博通和高通的交易也被阻止，我相信博通当时位于新加坡，正在搬到美国……（2018 年也发生了非常糟糕的事情，有人没有付给唐纳德足够的钱）

https://thediplomat.com/2014/02/india-inc-and-the-cfius-nati... 印度公司被迫从美国科技公司撤资......（2013）

我确信欧洲也有这样的例子，但如今规模较小的 OFC、人工智能公司估值过高，大多数收购在 2020 年代之前从未如此大规模……

我知道，除了公共股票或债券之外，大多数人都不想因为这个原因在美国投资。美国的私人外国投资一直很高，只是因为欧洲的养老金和中东的资金投入其中。

我不知道与这些相比有多公平、多远或多正确，拘留创始人也没有得到证实，但当然让我们假设它仍然是真的......

在美国唯一的区别是外国人可能可以对此提起诉讼。有时，如果他们幸运并且这笔交易值得的话。

我觉得很奇怪，居住在美国的 HN 人对他们的国家对外国公司所做的事情知之甚少，但却对外国公司对他们所做的事情感到愤怒？

我的意思是，世界上其他地方（96％）确实不存在，它只是一个神话或一块土地，美国的好人只想在需要时获取价值？

不确定这个评论的意思，顺便说一句，这以前也发生过，这些只是 2010 年代后的例子，因为它们是相关的。俄罗斯和美国也曾经这样做过，印度和美国在2000年代之前的情况更糟，日本和美国在1980年代在贸易和收购方面处于困境......

US has blocked merges of companies especially with Chinese and other non western companies. Including Japan, India etc.

For instance US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel(japanese) is one such example. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cx2vz83pg9eo

More examples,

Ant Group(chinese) tried to buy MoneyGram (blocked in 2018) https://www.reuters.com/article/business/us-blocks-moneygram...

Xiamen Sanan Optoelectronics tried buying Lumileds, blocked again by US. Also Chinese ofc.

Broadcom and Qualcomm deal was also blocked, Broadcom was then Singapore based in process of moving to US I believe... (very sus happened in 2018 too, someone didn't pay Donald enough)

https://thediplomat.com/2014/02/india-inc-and-the-cfius-nati... Indian company forced to divest from US tech firm... (2013)

I am certain there must be European examples as well but smaller ofc, AI companies are over valued these days, most acquisitions were never this big in the olden days of pre 2020s...

I know for a fact that most folks don't want to invest in US for this reason other than in public equities or bonds ofc. Private foreign investment in US has been high only due to European pensions and Middle-eastern money going into it.

I don't know about how fair, far, or right it was compared to these were, detaining founders is also not confirmed, but sure let's assume it's true still...

Only difference in US is perhaps foreign folks can sue over it. Sometimes, if they are lucky and if the deal is worth it.

I find it strange people of HN being based in US can be so ill informed of what their country, does to foreign companies but be mad about things foreign companies do to them?

I mean sure rest(96%) of the world doesn't really exist, it's but a myth or a land the better folks of US only want to take value when needed?

Unsure what this comment meant, this has happened before as well btw, these are just post 2010s examples because they are relevant. Russians and US used to do this too, India and US were worse of pre-2000s, Japan and US were at their throats in 1980s, in terms of trade and acquisition...

rvnx 2026/04/28 12:57
每个部门都有自己的法律（核、国防、军民两用技术等）。

把你想要的任何知识都记在脑子里。

现在想一想，如果你将这些想法从嘴里输出给你的朝鲜最好的朋友，会发生什么。

现在以色列最好的朋友也一样。

同样的法律，只是名单上多了一个条目，是政客而不是独立法院任意制定的。

Each sectors have their own laws for that (nuclear, defense, dual-use techs, etc).

Take any knowledge you’d like with you in your head.

Now think what is going to happen if you export these thoughts from your mouth to your North Korean best friend.

Now the same with Israeli best friend.

Same laws, just one extra entry on the list arbitrarily made by politicians, not independent courts.

在 HN 中查看
maxglute 2026/04/28 13:07
这不是他们的想法，而是在新加坡 switcheroo 之前由中国注册实体创建的核心技术，这使得他们的知识产权源自中国，并根据中国法律受中国管辖。对于实际法律，中华人民共和国2020年出口管制第13条。基本上是包罗万象的规定/全面禁止锤（对于人工智能代理等新技术的情况），即拒绝/禁止的推定。
It's not their thought, it's core technology created under PRC registered entities before Singapore switcheroo, which makes their IP PRC origin and under purview of PRC according to PRC law. For actual law, article 13 of PRC 2020 export control. Basically catchall provision / blanket ban hammer (for cases of new tech like AI agent), i.e. presumption of denial / ban.
在 HN 中查看
adrian_b 2026/04/28 15:29
美国已禁止Cadence/Synopsys公司的部分EDA软件向中国出口。

因此，美国的出口管制法显然将“思想”的出口定为非法。

一个更明显的例子是，如果任何美国公民知道机密信息，甚至只是某些私人公司的商业秘密，并将这些信息告诉中国，那么他就会做出违法的事情。

在本案中，中国辩称，知识产权是在中国创造的，将其转让给新加坡并不意味着它有资格转让给美国。

这与美国过去多次使用过的论点相同，例如禁止ASML向中国出售设备，禁止台积电的先进制造节点拥有中国客户，尽管事实上，在这两种情况下，前一段时间源自美国的IP仅占这些公司销售产品的很小一部分。

如果美国可以这样做，那么中国当然也有权这样做。这不是“左右主义”，但两国必须平等对待，要么国际法都禁止两国的此类行为，要么允许两国为所欲为。

毫无疑问，美国是发明这个概念的国家，它的法律可以在其领土之外适用，并且可以适用于非美国实体的财产，只要它们有任何组件，无论多小，这些组件最初是由美国实体直接或间接出售给它们的。

我认为任何此类法律解释都是滥用和荒谬的，但没有一个美国人可以批评一个除了模仿美国所做的事情之外什么也不做的外国。

USA has banned the export of some EDA software from Cadence/Synopsys to China.

Therefore the export-control laws of USA obviously make illegal the export of "thoughts".

An even more clear example if that any US citizen who knows classified information or even just a trade secret of some private company and who would tell that information to China would do something illegal.

In this case China argues that the IP has been created in China and its transfer to Singapore does not make it eligible for transfer to USA.

This is the same argument that USA has used multiple times in the past, e.g. for forbidding ASML to sell equipment to China and for forbidding TSMC to have Chinese customers for its advanced fabrication nodes, despite the fact that in both cases the IP that originated in USA some time ago was only a very small part of the products sold by those companies.

If USA may do this, then China is certainly also entitled to do the same. This is not whataboutism, but both countries must be treated equally, either such actions should be forbidden for both under the international laws, or they should be permitted both to do whatever they please.

There is absolutely no doubt that USA is the country who has invented this concept that its laws can be applied outside its territory and they can be applied to things that are the property of non-US entities, as long as they have any component, no matter how small, which has originally been sold to them, directly or indirectly, by an US entity.

I consider any legal interpretation of this kind as abusive and ridiculous, but no American may criticize a foreign country that does nothing else except imitate what USA does.

在 HN 中查看
8note 2026/04/28 07:06
> 问题是美国是否真的通过拘留公司所有者来阻止一家公司被出售到海外。

值得注意的是，中国也没有这样做：他们是禁止出境，而不是拘留，并且没有报道禁止出境的原因，所以说这是为了阻止公司在海外出售有些牵强。

美国：

- 提出广泛的管辖权主张 - 具有出口管制，这在文章中被列为阻止销售的潜在原因，并且 - 当想要确保某些人可以聊天时限制出境

我不明白推动这个具体案例有什么令人兴奋的。存在这样的错误：“谁试图通过将公司出售给外国对手来出口受控知识产权？”

我不明白这个案子有什么令人兴奋的，以至于美国绝对不会采取与中国非常相似的做法——让首席执行官在国会作证，并将他们留在国内，直到政府满意为止。到底是什么与众不同，让这一切如此有趣？这是工作合规课程的内容。

相反，你可能想回答哪家美国高科技国防承包商已被伊朗、中国、朝鲜或俄罗斯成功收购，并且美国已同意？

我预计无论哪种方式都缺乏数据。它不会出现，因为人们通常将公司迁往美国，而不是迁出

为什么要死在这座山上？

> it's whether USA has actually prevented a company from being sold overseas by detaining their owners.

notably china isnt doing this either: they are barring exit, not detaining, and the reason for barring exit was not reported, so its a stretch to say that its to prevent the sale of the company overseas.

The US:

- makes broad claims of jurisdiction - has export control, which is listed in the article as a potential reason for blocking the sale, and - restricts exit from the country when it wants to make sure certain people are available to chat

I dont see whats so exciting about pushing on this specific case. There's an error of, "who's tried to export controlled IP by selling their company to a foreign adversary?"

I dont see what's so exciting about this case that the US definitely absolutely wouldnt take a pretty similar approach to china - bring the CEOs to testify before congress and keep them in the country til the government is satisfied. What's so out of the ordinary that makes this interesting? This is the stuff that goes into work compliance courses.

you might instead want to answer which high tech defense contractor for the US has successfully been bought out by say, iran, china, north korea, or russia, that the US has given the OK on?

I expect there's a lack of data either way. It doesnt come up because people generally move their companies to the US, not out

why is this the hill to die on?

在 HN 中查看
maxglute 2026/04/28 06:29
美国的出口管制阻止公司出售受管制的技术。如果美国公司试图规避，那么他们绝对会被拒绝，如果他们秘密地这样做，违反法律，他们当然可能会被交出护照，即如果存在飞行风险，将被禁止出境。

这并不复杂，区别在于，在中国堵住漏洞并礼貌地示意他们停止后，马努斯完全智障，足以透明地规避中国的出口管制，但他们没有这么做，也就是说，他们违反了实际的出口管制法。就像马努斯没有试图出售一样，他们他妈的出售、签名和点缀，尽管被告知不要这样做，因为这违反了出口管制法。

即使是美国公司也很少如此公然密集。美国人因出售受控硬件而被禁止出境的情况没有马努斯试图做的那么严重，即在美国（相对）较少的出口管制犯罪得到同样的待遇。

US export controls prevent companies from selling controlled tech. If US companies tried o circumvent then they would absolutely be denied, if they did secretly anyway, against, the law of course they'll likely have passport surrendered, i.e. exit ban if flight risk.

Like this isn't complicated, the difference is Manus was full blown retarded enough to transparently circumvent PRC export controls after PRC closed loopholes and politely signalled them to stop, which they didn't, i.e. they broke actual export control laws. Like Manus didn't try to sell, they fucking sold, sign and dotted, despite being told not to, because its against export control laws.

Even US companies rarely this blatantly dense. Americans getting exit banned for selling controlled hardware is LESS serious then what Manus tried to do, i.e. lesser (relative) export control crimes in US getting same treatment.

在 HN 中查看
reissbaker 2026/04/28 11:05
你在说什么？具体差异如下：

1.美国有一个长期的、极其公开的政策，从2022年起就不能向中国出售Nvidia芯片。Supermicro是一家美国公司（总部位于加利福尼亚州圣何塞），他们从2024年到2025年向中国出售芯片，他们被抓了，所以他们被逮捕了。

2. Manus 创始人创建了……特工安全带？他们的公司是在新加坡注册的，而不是在中国。和 后 他们将新加坡公司出售给Meta，中国认定出售新加坡代理软件“违反了出口管制”（甚至中共代表也无法列出其违反了哪些所谓的管制），并将他们全部拘留在中国，并试图迫使新加坡公司取消出售。

这些其实没有可比性。超微公司的员工在美国经营一家公司，多年来他们就提前知道他们的行为违反了美国的出口管制。就马努斯而言，他们不是一家中国公司，没有人知道他们据称违反了不成文的出口管制，中国事后决定拘留他们所有人，并试图迫使这家（新加坡！）公司取消出售。

很简单，这在美国企业界从未发生过。美国对企业非常友好，你必须疯狂， 明知地 因并购交易错误而被捕。

What are you talking about? Here are the concrete differences:

1. The U.S. has had a long-standing, extremely public policy that you Cannot Sell Nvidia Chips to China since 2022. Supermicro is an American company (based in San Jose, California), and they sold chips to China from 2024-2025, and they got caught, so they were arrested.

2. Manus founders created... an agent harness? And their company was incorporated in Singapore, not in China. And after they sold their Singaporean company to Meta, China decided that selling Singaporean agentic software "violated export controls" (and even the CCP representative couldn't list which supposed control it violated), and detained them all in China and is attempting to force the Singaporean company to unwind the sale.

These are not really comparable. The Supermicro folks are running a company in America and knew ahead of time, for years, that what they were doing violated American export controls. In the case of Manus, they weren't a Chinese company, no one knew they were supposedly violating unwritten export controls, and China decided post-hoc to detain them all and attempt to force the (Singaporean!) company to unwind the sale.

Quite simply this has never happened in corporate America. America is very friendly to corporations and you'd have to be wildly, knowingly in the wrong to get arrested for an M&A deal.

在 HN 中查看
maxglute 2026/04/28 12:19
不，它们完全相同，只是 Manus 比 Nvidia 和 Supermicro 更迟钝。

1. 美国对可出口的计算层制定了出口管制政策。当政策不明确且计算阈值发生变化时，拜登政府在空位期发出了对“否认推定”的空白理解，而政策则找到了确切的控制措施。在情况明朗之前，英伟达已停止出口。这就是尽职调查/合规的含义。

2. Manus 在中华人民共和国创建了人工智能算法，因此属于中华人民共和国的管辖范围。解雇了他们的中国团队，并认为在新加坡成立公司是为了将中国控制的技术转移到美国以获得丰厚薪水的漏洞。在销售敲定之前，中国已向新加坡发出信号，该漏洞并非规避出口管制的法律陷阱。这是中华人民共和国版本的否认推定。最终，中国将决定在中国创建的 Manus 技术是否可以出口，以及在什么条件下可以出口。新加坡的中国公司没问题，用新加坡转账到美国……不行。马努斯收到的信号数量同样清晰。中国的著作谈到了中国出口管制第 13 条在销售之前很久就被触发。马努斯做了愚蠢和非法（叛国）的事情，并决定进行出售并强迫中华人民共和国出手，而尽职调查意味着你知道，不是。 AKA Manus 做了一个超微型但更令人震惊的。

美国如此友好，他们必须对中国进行出口管制……阻止美国人才在中国半阿米里工作。哦等等，事实证明，当涉及到国家安全/双重使用技术时，双方都对商业极其不友好，并且可以玩同样的地缘政治游戏。这种情况在美国并没有发生，因为坦率地说，由于美国政府的域外影响力，美国企业还没有落后到炫耀美国国家安全工具的程度。马努斯认为他们可以用中华人民共和国来做到这一点，因为中华人民共和国的治外法权范围较小，现在中华人民共和国就打了他们一巴掌作为例子。目前还没有人被捕，只是因正在进行的调查而被禁止出境。但如果他们最终被捕，也不会感到惊讶，因为他们明知是错的。

No they're exactly the same, except Manus more retarded than Nvidia and Supermicro.

1. US had wiffle waffle export control policies on what TIER of compute that was exportable. When policies were unclear and compute threshold shifted, Biden admin signalled blank understanding of "presumption of denial" in interregnum while policy figured out exact controls. Nvidia stopped exporting until clarity. That's what due diligence / compliance means.

2. Manus created AI algo in PRC and hence under PRC purview. Fired their PRC team and thought incorporating in Singapore was loophole to transfer PRC controlled tech to US for fat paycheck. PRC was signalled before sale finalizes the Singapore loophole was not some lawfare gotcha to circumvent export controls. This was PRC version of presumption of denial. Ultimately PRC gets to decide if Manus technology CREATED IN PRC is exportable, and under what conditions. PRC company in Singapore fine, using Singapore to transfer to US... not fine. The amount of signals Manus got was similarly clear. PRC writings were talking about art13 of PRC export controls being triggered long before sales. Manus did the retarded and illegal (treasonously) thing and decided to go through with sale and forced PRC hand, when due diligence meant you know, not. AKA Manus did a supermicro but more egregious.

America so friendly they have to export control PRC... prevent US talent from working in PRC semi amirite. Oh wait, turns out when it comes to national security / duo use tech both sides are wildly not business friendly and can play the same geopolitical game. This hasn't happened in America, because quite frankly US businesses are not retarded enough to flaunt US national security instruments due to US gov extraterritorial reach. Manus thought they can do so with PRC who has less extraterritorial = reach, and now PRC slapping them as example. Also no one's arrested yet, just exit ban for ongoing investigation. But would not be surprised if they do end up getting arrested, because they wildly, knowingly in the wrong.

stego-tech 2026/04/28 04:06
你说得对。据我所知，我们不会扣押公民作为人质，迫使他们将一家外国公司从美国走私到另一个国家，出售给另一家外国公司。

但是，当我们用我们的经济作为棍棒来对付任何与我们有一点分歧的人（对古巴、伊朗、中国、俄罗斯等的制裁；对 大家），在世界各地建立军事基地，以便在接到通知后立即入侵任何人，定期将我们的海军停泊在外国海岸附近以强制达到预期的结果，并用无人机袭击平民，作为全面冲突前的最后一次武力威胁。

细节发生了变化，但基本策略——利用国家暴力来胁迫有利于该国家的结果——远非新鲜事。 天哪，看看过去三十年的历史，有一个 过剩 帝国利用这种杠杆来取得成果的事件 - 包括美国！ 我们通过交换政治犯来实现谈判结果 反复，我们只是用不同的词语来让自己感觉更好。 我们支持了整个傀儡国家，以确保美国企业的利益得到满足！

喜欢， 天啊，为什么我必须教你们这些反对者历史书上已经概述的内容，只是因为你们懒得做指定的阅读？

You're right. To my knowledge, we don't hold citizens hostage to force them to unwind the sale of a foreign company they smuggled out of America into another country to a different foreign company.

But you cannot seriously hold America up as blameless when we've wielded our economy as a cudgel against anyone we remotely disagree with (sanctions against Cuba, Iran, China, Russia, etc; tariffs against everybody), have military bases scattered around the world to invade anyone at a moment's notice, regularly park our navy off foreign shores to coerce desired outcomes, and dronestrike civilians as a final saber-rattling before full-fledged conflict.

The details change, but the fundamental playbook - using state violence to coerce outcomes favorable to said state - is far from new. Hell, take a look beyond the past thirty years of history and there's a glut of incidents where empires used this sort of leverage to achieve outcomes - including the United States! We've traded political prisoners to achieve negotiated outcomes repeatedly, we just use different words to make ourselves feel better about it. We've propped up entire puppet states to ensure American corporate interests were served instead!

Like, holy shit, why do I have to teach you naysayers what's already outlined in history books just because you can't be bothered to do the assigned reading?

在 HN 中查看
stego-tech 2026/04/28 04:30
我的意思是，“Whataboutism”是否定宣传的一个重要工具。 而不是把重点放在应受谴责上 任何人 用这样的暴力威胁来迫使有利于国内政策的具体结果，每个人反而都挂在这个事实上 中国 做了这个。

有效使用的Whataboutism是为了进行类比而不是为行为找借口。 他妈的 中国在这里所做的事情，但也 他妈的 过去曾使用类似策略并取得巨大效果的国家和实体。 不要只是因为政府贴上“坏人”标签的人也这么做了，就对家里正在发生的事情视而不见。

I mean, the whataboutism is a critical tool in negating propaganda. Rather than focus on the reprehensibility of anyone using threats of violence like this to force specific outcomes favorable to domestic policy, everyone is instead hung up on the fact China did this.

Whataboutism, used effectively, is meant to draw parallels rather than excuse behavior. Fuck China for what it's doing here, but also fuck the countries and entities who have used similar tactics in the past to great effect. Don't just conveniently put on blinders for what's happening/happened at home all because the government-labelled "baddie" did it too.

在 HN 中查看
boc 2026/04/28 04:49
有效使用Whataboutism旨在改变主题并停止对当前主题的详细探索。这就是您在该线程中所做的事情。我们不需要将专门针对中共的新闻相关话题变成对美国政府和企业数十年行为进行重新诉讼的话题。如果您愿意，可以单独提交讨论美国的内容。
Whataboutism, used effectively, is designed to change the subject and stop detailed exploration of the topic at hand. Which is what you're doing in this thread. We don't need to turn a news-relevant thread specifically about the CCP into a thread relitigating decades of American government and business behavior. You can make a separate submission to discuss the US if you'd like.

pear01 2026/04/28 04:39
参议员汤姆·科顿（R-AR）：

所以你今天说的，正如你常说的，你住在新加坡。您是哪个国家的公民？

Shou Chew:

新加坡。

棉布：

您是其他国家的公民吗？

嚼：

不，参议员。

棉布：

您申请过中国国籍吗？

嚼：

参议员？我在新加坡为我的国家服务。不，我没有。

棉布：

你有新加坡护照吗？

嚼：

是的，我在新加坡服役了两年半。

棉布：

您还有其他国家的护照吗？

嚼：

不，参议员。

棉布：

你的妻子是美国公民。你的孩子是美国公民吗？

嚼：

这是正确的。

棉布：

您曾经申请过美国公民身份吗？

嚼：

不，还没有。

棉布：

好的。您曾经是中国共产党党员吗？

嚼：

参议员？我是新加坡人，不。

棉布：

您是否曾经与中国共产党有联系或隶属关系？

嚼：

不，参议员，我再说一遍，我是新加坡人。

Sen. Tom Cotton (R-AR):

So you said today, as you often say that you live in Singapore. Of what nation are you a citizen?

Shou Chew:

Singapore.

Cotton:

Are you a citizen of any other nation?

Chew:

No, Senator.

Cotton:

Have you ever applied for Chinese citizenship?

Chew:

Senator? I served my nation in Singapore. No, I did not.

Cotton:

Do you have a Singaporean passport?

Chew:

Yes, and I served my military for two and a half years in Singapore.

Cotton:

Do you have any other passports from any other nations?

Chew:

No, Senator.

Cotton:

Your wife is an American citizen. Your children are American citizens?

Chew:

That's correct.

Cotton:

Have you ever applied for American citizenship?

Chew:

No, not yet.

Cotton:

Okay. Have you ever been a member of the Chinese Communist Party?

Chew:

Senator? I'm Singaporean, no.

Cotton:

Have you ever been associated or affiliated with the Chinese Communist Party?

Chew:

No. Senator, again, I'm Singaporean.

IncreasePosts 2026/04/28 06:59

在 HN 中查看
pear01 2026/04/28 07:32
美国是什么种族？所有其他种族是否都应该在美国国会接受同样一轮的质询？

我不知道你为什么要捍卫汤姆·科顿的智力。在法庭或国会场合提问的第一条规则是预期答案。更强烈地说，人们常说你不应该问一个你还不知道答案的问题。如果他认为他会引起中国人的一些“民族主义”反应，那么他就失败了，因此，他提出这样的质疑是愚蠢的。我同意你的观点，他知道新加坡不在中国。这并不是让他的提问变得愚蠢的原因。本质上，他多次问同一个问题并得到相同的答案。他之所以看起来很蠢，是因为他的提问方式很蠢。

如果他有证据证明这位首席执行官是“民族主义者”，他就可以质问他。他没有，也没有，所以他反而提出了一些废话，最终让他感到尴尬。如果像你所说的那样有一个未说明的问题，他可以直接问。他没有。这是为什么？

他这样的质问，除了让自己看起来像个傻子之外，还能达到什么目的呢？这是他的一份工作，他是仅有的 100 个人（真的更少）之一获得这些机会，这是他能做的最好的事情吗？你为什么要为他找借口？对您的代表提出更好的要求。

我应该指出，我真的很努力地对你的观点表示宽容。我认为这里真正未明说的、明显的潜台词是一个来自阿肯色州的姓“科顿”的白人正在公开交易同类型的种族主义狗，他的祖先很可能参与过吹口哨。我的意思是，如果我们只是随意指责人们是民族主义者，为什么不从参议员开始呢？既然你认为根据你相当宽泛的陈述“有些人是民族主义者”（好吧……并且？）来制定调查路线没有问题，那么也许我们应该从参议员本人开始。我的意思是，为什么不呢？是什么让 Tiktok 首席执行官成为更引人注目的嫌疑人？我认为这段视频除了微弱的质疑之外还引起了共鸣，这是显而易见的——因为许多美国人在这起案件中能够同情这位首席执行官。如果参议员做了基本功课，他就会知道新加坡不允许双重公民，所以他在第一个问题上已经有了答案，如果他做了任何基础研究，他就会知道这一点。他们应该为这些你知道的事情做好准备。

我的意思是，作为一名美国公民，我真的对你感到失望。如果有人是民族主义者，你需要让你的代表在美国提出此类问题……这感觉很讽刺。您可能应该阅读一下您的历史，大多数在美国参议院权力席位上提出此类问题的人在历史上都是民族主义者。正如我所说，如果科顿有证据证明他的观点，他本可以提出这些观点。或者直接询问他们的情况。相反，他多次问过关于他的公民身份的同一问题。为什么？

不过，我很感谢你的回复，我认为这是善意的。我并不是想把它变成一个单纯的种族/民族问题，我认为这只会疏远你。再说一遍，你是提出“民族主义”的人。我什至不确定我知道你所说的这个术语到底是什么意思，但我发现你在这里引用它是值得怀疑的。但我试图对你的立场保持宽容。重点仍然是他的提问并没有澄清任何“民族主义”的概念，但老实说，我觉得我必须编辑这个并更直接地批评你的反驳。我只是认为你可能想考虑为什么这个剪辑能引起共鸣，而不是你似乎假设的稻草人（互联网认为科顿认为新加坡在中国）。

What is the American ethnicity? Should all other ethnicities be subjected to the same round of questioning before the United States Congress?

I'm not sure why you are defending Tom Cotton's intelligence. Rule #1 of asking questions in a courtroom or a congressional setting is to anticipate the answers. Put more strongly, it is often said you should not ask a question you don't already know the answer to. If he thought he was going to elicit some Chinese "ethnonationalist" response, then he failed, and as such, was idiotic in pursuing this line of questioning. I agree with you he knows Singapore is not in China. That's not what makes his line of questioning stupid. It is that he essentially asks the same question multiple times and gets the same answer. The reason he looks dumb is because his line of questioning is dumb.

If he had some evidence the CEO was an "ethnonationalist" he could have confronted him with that. He doesn't and he didn't, so instead he committed himself to a bs line of questioning that ended up embarrassing him. If there is an unstated question as you claim, he could have asked it directly. He didn't. Why is that?

What did he achieve by this line of questioning, besides making himself look like a fool? This is his one job, he's one of only 100 people (really less) who gets these opportunities and this is the best he can do? Why are you making excuses for him? Demand better from your representatives.

I should note I am really straining to be charitable to your view. I think the real unstated, obvious subtext here is a white guy from Arkansas with the last name "Cotton" is openly trading in the same type of racist dog whistling his ancestors more than likely engaged in. I mean if we are just going to randomly accuse people of being ethnonationalists why not start with the Senator? Since you see no problem with crafting lines of inquiry based on your rather broad statement that "some people are ethnonationalists" (ok... and?) then maybe we should start with the Senator himself. I mean, why not? What makes the Tiktok CEO a more compelling suspect? I think it's obvious why the clip resonated beyond the feeble questioning - it's because many Americans can empathize with the CEO in this case. If the Senator had done his basic homework he would know Singapore doesn't allow dual citizens, so he already had his answer at the first question, which he would have already known if he had done any basic research. They are supposed to prepare for these things you know.

I mean I really am just disappointed in you, as an American citizen. The idea you need to have your representatives ask these kinds of questions in the United States on the off chance someone is an ethnonationalist... it just feels ironic. You should probably read up on your history most people who ask these types of questions from the seats of power in the United States Senate have historically been the ethnonationalists. As I stated if Cotton had evidence of his views, he could have raised them. Or asked about them directly. Instead he essentially asked the same question about his citizenship numerous times. Why?

I appreciate you replying in what I take to be good faith though. I don't mean to turn it into a question of race/ethnicity alone, which I gather will only alienate you. Then again, you are the one who brought up "ethnonationalism". I'm not even sure I know what exactly you mean by that term, but I find your invocation of it here to be suspect. But I am trying to be charitable to your position. The point remains his line of questioning did not clear up any "ethnonationalist" notions, but honestly I felt I had to edit this and be more straightforward with my criticism of your rejoinder. I just think you might want to consider why this clip resonated, instead of the straw man you seemed to posit (internet thinks Cotton thinks Singapore is in China).

在 HN 中查看
mothballed 2026/04/28 08:07
事实上，你不应该问你不知道答案的问题，这是司法和立法体系遭到破坏的标志之一。 这是一个不利于好奇和好奇推理的原则。
The fact you shouldn't ask questions you don't know the answer to is one of the indicators that the judicial and legislative systems is broken. That's a principle that is hostile to inquisitive and curious reasoning.
在 HN 中查看
pear01 2026/04/28 08:30
盘问或国会听证会不是大学讲座。

你愿意让你的自由受到威胁，以便法官或参议员能够以你为代价来满足他们的好奇心吗？您知道不遵守司法或国会传票会受到什么处罚吗？伪证罪的处罚是否符合“好奇推理”？或者说这是一种“敌意”的工具？

如果司法和立法制度等同于"好奇和好奇的推理"，那么它就不是一个自由的国家。如果他们想履行这一职能，他们可以放弃剥夺人民自由的权力。

a cross examination or a congressional hearing is not a university lecture.

would you like your liberty to be at risk just so a judge or a senator can satisfy their curiosity at your expense? do you have any idea what the penalties can be for failing to comply with a judicial or congressional subpoena? is the penalty of perjury consistent with "inquisitive and curious reasoning"? or is that an instrument of "hostility"?

it would not be a free country if the judicial and legislative systems were equated with "inquisitive and curious reasoning". if they want to serve that function they can give up their power to deprive people of their liberty.

在 HN 中查看
mothballed 2026/04/28 08:40
如果你想翻转脚本并攻击传票，当然可以。 非自愿传票权本身就是敌对的。 这是契约奴役或临时奴役的形式，甚至没有犯罪或不当行为的指控。 我认为这在道德上是令人憎恶的，我并不主张对那些甚至没有受到不当行为起诉的人使用暴力强制传票的权力。 这些传票本身并不是你自豪地使用“自由”这个词的表现。

我不明白你的言论是在攻击我的言论。 您正在攻击非自愿传票权。 我不同意你的观点。 这是一个有趣的转移注意力的话题，我发现它是一个有趣的话题，所以我很乐意讨论它，但不要假装你削弱了我的论点。 但摆脱传票权并仍然拥有司法或立法权也并非不可能，即使你认为司法系统的效率会降低或类似（我个人认为“自由”的好处超过传票的好处）。

If you want to flip the script and attack subpoenas, sure. Involuntary subpoena power is hostile in and of itself. It's form of indentured servitude or temporary slavery without even an accusation of crime or wrongdoing. I think it's morally abhorrent and I am not advocating for violence enforced subpoena power of those not even under indictment of misconduct to exist. These subpoenas themselves are not an exhibition of being 'free' as you proudly use the word.

I don't see how what you're saying as attacking what I'm saying. You're attacking involuntary subpoena power. I don't disagree with you. It's an interesting red herring, and I find it an interesting topic, so I'm happy to discuss it but not under the pretenses you are weakening my argument. But it's not impossible to get rid of subpoena power and still have judicial or legislative powers, even if you argue the judicial system will be less effective or some such (personally I think the benefits of being 'free' outweigh the advantages of subpoena).

在 HN 中查看
pear01 2026/04/28 08:50
抱歉，我根本不知道你在说什么。如果你试图提出一些替代系统，那是一回事。我只是澄清一下，您的说法在我们已有的系统背景下没有意义。这并不意味着要转移注意力。审判律师中之所以存在“不要问问题……”的格言，是因为国家对其公民（以及它有权管辖的其他人）拥有巨大的权力。

现在我想也许你的意思是国家不应该拥有这些权力，因此我们应该能够更自由地在法庭和国会提出和回答问题，然后就好了。但我不确定我是否同意这一点，或者我想参与您提出的任何替代方案。我认为，将某种“对好奇和好奇的推理的敌意”等同于这句格言是不合适的，因为重点是法院不是发生这种事情的场所。

现在，如果你认为它们应该是，那是一个单独的论点。我不明白任何有权剥夺人们自由的机构怎么可能成为“好奇推理”的场所。这就是为什么我说法院和国会不是大学。事实上，有非常好的历史文献阐明了大学作为西方传统中的主权实体的作用，因为它与国家机构之间几乎是定义上的紧张关系。

在阅读您评论中的潜台词时，我认为我们可能在多个领域达成共识，但我们只是从稍微不同的方向来实现这一点。再次，这句格言是针对科顿作为考官的表现而出现的，因为处于他地位的人有很大的权力去伤害坐在他面前的人。

这是一个设计上的对抗性系统。如果你想重新设计它，那么这句话是有道理的，因为这就是系统。在这种情况下，这也适用于科顿，即使他是这里的国家，因为自然地，即使对他不会产生任何真正的后果，如果你提出问题而不考虑你将引出的答案，他仍然会面临与在这种场合成功进行论证相同的风险。我也不认为他多次问关于他的公民身份的基本相同的问题是一种“好奇和好奇的推理”。因此，虽然我认为我可能会同情你的一般观念，但我不确定我是否真的知道你的意思。

Sorry, I simply don't know what point you are making. If you are trying to propose some alternative system that is one thing. I'm merely clarifying that your statement does not make sense in the context of the system we already have. It is not meant to be a red herring. The reason that adage about "don't ask questions..." even exists among trial lawyers is because of the state's great power over its own citizens (and others it has authority over).

Now I guess maybe what you are saying is that the state shouldn't have these powers, and therefore we should be able to more freely ask and answer questions in courts and congress, then fine. But I'm not sure I agree with that or I want to engage with whatever you proposed alternative is. I think simply it doesn't comport to equate some kind "hostility to inquisitive and curious reasoning" to the adage, because the point is that the courts are not a venue for such a thing.

Now if you think they should be, that is a separate argument. I don't see how any institution that has the power to deprive people of their liberty could ever be a venue for "inquisitive and curious reasoning". Which is why I said the courts and congress are not the universities. In fact, there is very good historical literature that elucidates the role of the university as something of a sovereign entity in the Western tradition given this almost definitional tension with the institutions of the state.

In reading the subtext in your comment, I think we may agree in more than a few areas, but we are just coming at this from slightly different directions. Again, the adage came up with respect to Cotton's performance as an examiner in the context where people in his position have great power to damage those sitting before him.

It's an adversarial system by design. If you want to redesign it fine, but the adage makes sense given this is the system. It applies to Cotton in this case as well, even if he is the state here, because naturally even though there will not be any real consequences for him, he still falls under the same risks re the success of making an argument in this kind of venue if you ask a question without considering the answer you will be eliciting. I also don't really think him asking essentially the same question about his citizenship multiple times is a species of "inquisitive and curious reasoning" anyway. So while I think I may sympathize with your general notions I'm not sure I really know what you are getting at.

giancarlostoro 2026/04/28 03:11
有趣的是，当你认为世界的人工智能进步要归功于 Meta 和谷歌时，他们的开放版本确实改变了事情，如果我错了，请随时纠正我，特别是对于中国，据我所知，中国在人工智能方面的发布没有以前那么多。我记得当 Meta 发布 Llama 时，最初人们对它进行了猜测，但它最终产生了很多使用它的项目，我确信在中国有一些项目。我知道 Perplexity 在 Llama 之上有自己的自定义模型，他们将其用作默认模型，而且非常好。
Funny when you consider the world owes a lot of AI advancements to both Meta and Google, their open releases really did shift things, feel free to correct me if I'm wrong, especially for China, which as far as I know were not releasing as much in AI as they have been beforehand. I remember when Meta released Llama originally people were speculating about it, but it wound up producing a lot of projects that used it, I'm sure some in China. I know that Perplexity has its own custom model on top of Llama that they use for their default model, and its pretty darn good.

henry2023 2026/04/28 03:20
难道 Llama 不是一个如此受欢迎的泄密者决定改变他们的整个方法吗？

当时我在谷歌工作。在 Llama 之前，释放重量甚至不值得讨论。

Wasn’t Llama a leak that got so popular meta decided to change their whole approach?

I was working at Google at the time. Before Llama, releasing weights was not even worth a discussion.

在 HN 中查看
calebkaiser 2026/04/28 03:58
如果我没记错的话，事情比这更奇怪，因为 Llama 最初的发布策略有点奇怪。

您确实必须申请访问权限，但如果您符合他们的标准（基本上，如果您是研究人员或政府的合适人选），您就可以直接访问模型权重，而不仅仅是托管模型的 API 。因此访问受到限制，但全部权重是共享的。

我相信该模型被多个人泄露，其中一些人在 Meta 上不起作用，但已被授予访问权重的权限。

If I'm remembering right, it was weirder than that, as Llama's originally release strategy was sort of bizarre.

You did have to apply for access, but if you met their criteria (basically if you were the right profile of researcher or in government), you got direct access to the model weights, not just an API for a hosted model. So access was restricted, but the full weights were shared.

I believe that the model was leaked by multiple people, some of which didn't work at Meta but had been granted access to the weights.

在 HN 中查看
cma 2026/04/28 14:11
我不认为这是一个很大的泄露，也没有进行任何重要的验证——我认为 .edu 电子邮件会自动获得批准。它可能只是在那里，因为与它相关的版权法当时更加不确定，但他们有更强大的公平用例，并有学术/研究门。
I don't think it was much of a leak, there was no significant verification--I think .edu email approved automatically. It was probably just there because copyright law related to it was more uncertain then, but they had stronger fair use case with an academic/research gate on it.
在 HN 中查看
giancarlostoro 2026/04/28 03:29
不确定，但开放式举重已经产生了效果。例如，看看最后一个开放权重 Wan 版本的 Wan 2.2，它仍然是最强大的视频推理，就其提供的质量水平而言，不幸的是，它变成了闭源，但在此之前，社区已经在其之上构建了各种工具和 LoRas。一年后，没有任何视频可以与之相媲美。回到 llama，看看人们通过 Mac 离线运行的所有开放模型。它绝对具有净积极意义。
Not sure, but open weights have had their effects. For example, look at Wan 2.2 the last open weights Wan release, still the most powerful Video inference out there, to the level of quality it provides, unfortunately, it went closed source, but before they did, the community had built all sorts of tooling and LoRas on top of it. Nothing comes close for video a year later. Back to llama though, look at all the open models people run offline through their Macs. It definitely had a net positive.

更不用说他们在南海和台湾的公开扩张主义了。

strangegecko 2026/04/28 15:56
我不知道，对我来说这不是核心问题。

我不喜欢任何政党的做法，但从道德角度来看，一个人的宣传越独裁，我就越觉得具有侵略性和暴力性。

我认为中共的所作所为否认了我（和其他人）的人性和个人权利。我根本无法接受一个监禁艺术家和人权活动人士的政府。我觉得一个艺术就是犯罪的世界不值得生活在其中。

I have no idea and for me it's not the central question.

I don't like it from any party, but from a moral standpoint, the more authoritarian someone is about their propaganda, the more invasive and violent it feels to me.

I perceive what the CCP is doing as denying my (and other people's) humanity and individual rights. I simply cannot accept a government that imprisons artists and human rights activists. A world where art is crime is not one I find worth living in.

I perceive American propaganda in the same category as advertising, harmful and annoying, but they won't lock me up or threaten my safety if I speak up against the propaganda. Well, at least that's how it used to be, who knows with the current trajectory.

中国至少是秘密地这样做的。美国总统在推特上播放了他的疯子叙述，供所有人观看。
China at least does it covertly. The US president broadcasts his madman narrative on Twitter for everyone to see.
在 HN 中查看
strangegecko 2026/04/28 15:59
就我个人而言，我更喜欢美国的做法。至少这样每个人都知道他们正在处理什么，并且他们可以公开做出反应，而不是被迫在所说的和真正所说的之间进行虚假的舞蹈。

这些隐蔽的东西提供了某种程度的貌似合理的推诿，并导致大量民众沾沾自喜并忽视现实。除了那些进行宣传的人之外，我不认为这对任何人都有好处。

Personally, I prefer the US approach. At least then everyone knows what they're dealing with and they can openly react instead of being forced into a fake dance between what is said and what is REALLY said.

The covert stuff gives some degree of plausible deniability and it causes a good amount of the population to be complacent and ignore reality. I don't see how this can be considered good for anyone but the people creating propaganda.

burnerRhodov2 2026/04/28 09:25

stego-tech 2026/04/28 04:25
好的！

* 美国和英国支持伊朗国王以帮助西方石油利益： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1953_Iranian_coup_d状态

* 美国出口管制基本上束缚了任何参与为国家创造任何有价值物品的进口商： https://www.investopedia.com/u-s-export-restrictions-6753407

*我们继续对古巴实施禁运，而不是让它的成败取决于其自身的优点——同时还控制着他们自己的土地用于黑色行动监狱，并多次试图暗杀他们的领导人或发动政变： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_embargo_against_...

* 我们全球金融的集中化和储备货币的地位使我们能够决定从知识产权到国防等各个方面的全球政策，这意味着公司通常必须“合作”，否则东道国将受到处罚

* 那一次我们推翻了危地马拉的民主选举政府，因为他们强加了最低工资等激进的想法： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1954_Guatemalan_coup_d%27état

* 那一次，我们推翻了智利民选的社会主义政府，以支持剥削性的劳工行为和资源开采： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1973_Chilean_coup_d%27état

我可以继续说下去，但实际上，维基百科是 就在那里。 如果您正在寻找“我们绑架了首席执行官并要求一家外国公司取消合并”的特定类比，我认为我无法立即提供；然而，如果您正在寻找“国家使用威胁和武力来煽动有利于其国内政策的结果”的例子， 那么好吧, 男孩你好 是否有大量的例子，只需粗略搜索即可。

Okay!

* The US and UK propped up the Iranian Shah to help western oil interests: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1953_Iranian_coup_d'état

* US Export Controls basically handcuff anyone of import involved in creating anything of value to the state: https://www.investopedia.com/u-s-export-restrictions-6753407

* We continue to embargo Cuba instead of letting it succeed or fail on its own merits - while also controlling their own land for a Black Ops prison and having attempted repeatedly to assassinate their leaders or create coups: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_embargo_against_...

* Our centralization of global finance and status as a reserve currency lets us dictate global policy on everything from Intellectual Property to National Defense, meaning companies generally have to "play ball" or the host country will incur penalties

* That time we overthrew the democratically-elected government of Guatemala because they imposed radical ideas like a minimum wage: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1954_Guatemalan_coup_d%27état

* And that time we overthrew the democratically-elected socialist government in Chile to prop up exploitative labor practices and resource extraction: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1973_Chilean_coup_d%27état

I can go on, but really, Wikipedia is right there. If you're looking for a specific analogue to "we kidnapped CEOs and demanded a foreign company unwind their merger", I don't think I can provide that right away; however, if instead you're looking for examples of "country used threats and force to foment an outcome favorable to its domestic policies", well then, boy howdy are there tons and tons of examples out there just a cursory search away.

stego-tech 2026/04/28 04:09
哦，不，中国的做法令人难以置信地应受谴责。

就像美国支持伊朗国王以确保西方石油利益得到满足一样应受到谴责。 值得谴责的是，大英帝国让中国人沉迷于鸦片，以强迫签订更有利的贸易协定。 同样应受到谴责的是美国对古巴实施的封锁，它禁止古巴依靠自己的力量繁荣或失败，并将苦难强加给古巴人民。

它是 全部 应受谴责，而且应该 全部 不要让人们误以为美国是绝对不会犯错的救世主。 它是 坏的，它永远不应该发生，但它 做 和它 将要 只要人们继续相信这样的民族主义叙事。

Oh, no, it's incredibly reprehensible what China's doing.

Just like it was reprehensible that America propped up the Iranian shah to ensure western oil interests were served. And reprehensible that the British Empire got the Chinese addicted to Opium to force more favorable trade agreements. Also reprehensible is the Cuban blockade imposed by America, which has prohibited the country from thriving or failing on its own merits and forced suffering onto its people.

It's all reprehensible, and it should all be held up lest folks get this notion that America is this infallible savior who can do no wrong. It's bad, and it should never happen, but it does and it will so long as people keep buying into Nationalist narratives like these.

adithyan-rk 2026/04/28 14:21
交易完成后，“废止”意味着什么？权重、IP 和工程师都已经在 Meta 内部了。北京可以强迫创始人签署他们想要的任何文件，他们实际上在那里，但你无法收回已经集成的代码。 这是一个信号举措。真正的影响在于下一笔交易：任何着眼于新加坡或特拉华州转型的中国创始人现在都必须将“国家召唤你回家”视为真正的风险。印度在 2022-23 年有一个较为温和的版本，当时印度央行的规则迫使 PhonePe、Groww 和 Razorpay 在 IPO 之前逆转。交易完成后，“废止”意味着什么？权重、IP 和工程师都已经在 Meta 内部了。北京可以强迫创始人签署他们想要的任何文件，他们实际上在那里，但你无法收回已经集成的代码。 这是一个信号举措。真正的影响在于下一笔交易：任何着眼于新加坡或特拉华州转型的中国创始人现在都必须将“国家召唤你回家”视为真正的风险。印度在 2022-23 年有一个较为温和的版本，当时印度储备银行的规则迫使 PhonePe、Groww 和 Razorpay 在 IPO 之前逆转。
What does "annulment" even mean once the deal has closed? The weights, IP, and engineers are already inside Meta. Beijing can force the founders to sign whatever they want, they're physically there, but you can't claw back code that's already been integrated. This is a signaling move. The real impact is on the next deal: any China-origin founder eyeing a Singapore or Delaware flip now has to price in "state summons you home" as a real risk. India had a softer version in 2022–23 when RBI rules pushed PhonePe, Groww, and Razorpay to reverse-flip back before IPO.What does "annulment" even mean once the deal has closed? The weights, IP, and engineers are already inside Meta. Beijing can force the founders to sign whatever they want, they're physically there, but you can't claw back code that's already been integrated. This is a signaling move. The real impact is on the next deal: any China-origin founder eyeing a Singapore or Delaware flip now has to price in "state summons you home" as a real risk. India had a softer version in 2022–23 when RBI rules pushed PhonePe, Groww, and Razorpay to reverse-flip back before IPO.

sad day to be a chinese founder.

mullingitover 2026/04/28 06:12
> 中国政府对交易的干预引起了该国科技创始人和风险投资家的警惕，他们希望利用所谓的新加坡清洗模式，即公司从中国迁往新加坡，以避免北京和华盛顿的审查。

如果美国将人工智能技术视为战略问题并打国家安全牌，那么当中国也这样做时，就不会真正开始紧握珍珠。

看起来马努斯和梅塔认为他们会很聪明，中国不会打他们手里的任何牌。甚至 GPT-2 也可以告诉他们这是一个愚蠢的想法。

> The Chinese government’s intervention in the transaction drew alarm among tech founders and venture capitalists in the country who were hoping to take advantage of the so-called Singapore-washing model, where companies relocate from China to the city-state to avoid scrutiny from Beijing and Washington.

If the US is going to treat AI technology as a strategic issue upon which it's going to play the national security card, it's not really valid to start the pearl-clutching when China does the same.

Seems like Manus and Meta thought they were going to be clever, and that China wasn't going to play any any of the cards they were holding. Even GPT-2 could've told them that was a dumb idea.

众所周知，中共非常咄咄逼人。即使创始人获得了新加坡公民身份（这对于华裔来说比其他种族更容易），如果他们只是像出差一样踏上中国，中共也会将他们扣为人质。他们可以编造犯罪行为，并按照中国的规则接受审判。新加坡能做什么？这是一个试图同时与中国和美国保持良好关系的小国。
The CCP is known to be very aggressive. Even if the founders acquired Singaporean citizenship (which is way easier for ethnic Chinese people than for other races), the CCP would have taken them hostage if they just set foot in China like for a business trip. They can invent a crime and subject them to a trial with Chinese rules. What can Singapore do? It’s a tiny country that tries to walk a tightrope by simultaneously maintaining good relations with both China and the U.S.

在 HN 中查看
hug 2026/04/28 08:56
我对此进行了一些研究，因为我很好奇谁在这里更正确。

来自维基百科，它链接到似乎相对可靠的来源：

> 新加坡统计局在 CMIO 模型下将“华人”与“马来人、印度人和其他人”一起广泛定义为“种族”或“族裔群体”。 [10]他们包括“华裔”，如福建人、潮州人、海南人、广东人、客家人、兴化人、福州人、上海人、北方人等。”[11]

所以，综合考虑，我认为 kccqzy 的意思就是他们所说的，而且是非常正确的。

I dug a little into this because I was curious who was more correct here.

From wikipedia, which links to what seems like a relatively reliable source:

> The Singapore Department of Statistics broadly defines "Chinese" as a "race" or "ethnic group", in conjunction with "Malay, Indian and Others" under the CMIO model.[10] They consist of "persons of Chinese origin" such as the Hokkiens, Teochews, Hainanese, Cantonese, Hakka, Henghuas, Hokchias/Foochows, Shanghainese and Northern Chinese, etc."[11]

So I would, on the balance of things, think that kccqzy meant what they said, and was pretty correct about it.

在 HN 中查看
some_random 2026/04/28 03:50
中国公司的一个问题是，由于国际法律或光学原因，中国公司搬迁到新加坡，但仍由中国公民或除中国公民以外的所有公民控制。字节跳动就是一个很好的例子。俄罗斯公司对瑞士也做了同样的事情，请参阅卡巴斯基。
Chinese company has an issue being a Chinese company for international legal or optics reasons, relocates to Singapore while still being controlled by Chinese nationals or all-but-Chinese-Nationals. Bytedance is a great example. Russian companies do the same thing with Switzerland, see Kaspersky.
在 HN 中查看
dublinstats 2026/04/28 04:06
为此，他们也可以搬到加利福尼亚。

问题是它们是否仍受中华人民共和国控制。中国不允许双重国籍（像其他亚洲国家一样），因此人们可能合法地希望在保留本国护照的同时出国工作。

They could just as well relocate to California for that matter.

The question is are they still controlled by the PRC. China doesn't allow dual citizenship (like other Asian countries), so people might legitimately want to work abroad while keeping their native passport.

在 HN 中查看
BobbyJo 2026/04/28 07:45
这真的很难说。几乎所有模型的训练数据中都有基准，这将我们带入基于振动的模型能力排名领域。我认为 OSS 模型在语料库之外的事情上往往做得更糟，但 Deepseek 在效率和扩展方面做得非常好，这是可以通过能力无法验证的方式进行验证的。
It's really hard to tell. Almost all the models have the benchmarks in their training data, which pushes us into the realm of basing model capability rankings on vibes. I think the OSS models tend to do worse on things outside their corpus, but Deepseek specifically has done insanely good work on efficiency and scaling, which is verifiable in a way capabilities are not.

umeshunni 2026/04/28 04:49
> B2B 销售将与面向消费者的销售一样受到限制。

他们不是。 Temu、AliExpress、Shein 和其他中国出口商的广告支出的很大一部分都在 Meta 的平台上： https://www.cnbc.com/2024/01/31/metas-continued-rally-could-...

> b2b sales would be just as restricted as sales to consumers.

They're not. A significant part of ad spend by the likes of Temu, AliExpress, Shein and other Chinese exporters are on Meta's platforms: e.g. https://www.cnbc.com/2024/01/31/metas-continued-rally-could-...

culi 2026/04/28 13:57
这并不比我提出美国自己的集中营更有意义。

2024 年 8 月，美国公民自由联盟发布了一份关于这些营地的报告，名为《抵抗、报复、镇压：加州移民拘留两年》。

以下是它强调的一些问题：

- 为了吃饭而强迫劳动。每天 1 美元的“志愿工作计划”是您获得足够生活食物的唯一途径。如果您拒绝工作或试图抗议，ICE 无需找借口将您单独监禁。 CoreCivic 将这些劳动力出售给公司

- 广泛使用单独监禁，往往是为了“轻微的违纪行为或作为对参加绝食或提出申诉的报复”

- 有数十人因强迫劳动和医疗疏忽而死亡

毫无疑问，自本报告发布以来，这些问题只会变得更加严重。

https://www.aclunorcal.org/publications/resistance-retaliati...

美国关押着世界上25%的监狱人口。它是世界上少数几个因入狱而被剥夺投票权的国家之一。囚犯还要交“房租”，却没有赚钱的途径。如果他们真的逃脱了，他们的余生都会背负债务——这增加了他们再次参与这个营利性计划的机会

That's not any more relevant than if I were to bring up the US' own concentration camps.

In August of 2024, the ACLU released a report on these camps called "Resistance, Retaliation, Repression: Two Years in California Immigration Detention".

Here's some of the issues it highlighted:

- forced labor in order to afford to eat. The $1/day "Voluntary Work Program" is the only way you're gonna get enough food to live. And if you refuse to work or try to protest, ICE doesn't have to give an excuse to send you into solitary confinement. CoreCivic sells this labor to companies

- extensive use of solitary confinement often for "minor disciplinary infractions or as a form of retaliation for participating in hunger strikes or for submitting complaints"

- dozens of documented deaths from forced labor and medical neglect

No doubt these issues have only gotten worse since the publication of this report.

https://www.aclunorcal.org/publications/resistance-retaliati...

The US holds 25% of the world's prison population. It is one of the few countries in the world where someone's voting rights can be taken away for being inprisoned. Prisoners also have to pay "rent" but don't have a way of making money. If they do ever get out, they're saddled in debt for the rest of their lives—increasing the chance they go right back in to this for-profit scheme

看看规范欧洲公司注册的“第 28 条制度”，它早在 11 月份就已宣布，今年可能不会出现。我们不能再等了，我们需要现在就采取行动，以免变得完全无关紧要，甚至可能为时已晚。

EU is on the right path, but the problem is that we’re going way too slow.

Check out the “28th regime” that standardizes incorporation for European companies, it was announced back in November, and it won’t see the light this year probably. We can’t wait any second more, we need to act now not to become totally irrelevant and it might even be too late.

hasbr12 2026/04/28 04:52
美国对世界能源路线的控制可以追溯到苏伊士运河危机，当时它从英国手中夺走了运河。里根炸毁了俄罗斯-德国管道。北溪的破坏活动至少得到了宽恕和欢呼。现在，霍尔木兹海峡的关闭首先是美国挑起的，然后是美国拉拢的。

是的，这是关于世界其他地方的。

US controlling the world's energy routes goes back to the Suez Crisis, where it wrestled the canal from Britain. Reagan blew up a Russian-German pipeline. The Nord Stream sabotage was at least condoned and cheered on. Now the closure of Hormuz was first provoked and then co-opted by the US.

Yes, this is about the rest of the world.

在 HN 中查看
freakynit 2026/04/28 10:05
政府需要对私营企业保持距离，除非私营企业直接或间接地利用不法行为来限制自己的竞争。

政客需要受到控制，否则他们就会成为独裁者。如果没有，那么这种转变只是何时发生的问题，而不是是否发生的问题。

The governments need to keep their noses away from private companies, unless private companies turn to directly, or indirectly, limit their own competition using nefarious practises.

Politicians need to be kept in check, or else, they become dictators. If not, then it's just a matter of when, not if, that that transition happens.

在 HN 中查看
freakynit 2026/04/28 10:15
理想情况下，是的。

问题是，政府的此类行动通常是从正确的意图开始，范围非常有限……但是，随着时间的推移，两者都会因为对整个生态系统拥有更多的政治控制而被放弃，从而导致腐败蔓延，在更极端的情况下，非法逮捕和账户冻结。

问题始终是人性：权力导致腐败，绝对权力导致绝对腐败。

Ideally, yes.

The thing is such actions from govt.'s usually start with the right intent and in a very limited scope... but, with time, both gets traded away for having more political control over entire ecosystem, thereby, allowing corruption to creep-in, and on a more extreme level, illegal arrests and account freezes.

The issue is always the human nature: power corrupts, and absolute power corrupts absolutely.
